description: A salad built around cooked or roasted beets, often balanced with acidic dressing and creamy, crunchy, or peppery components such as feta, goat cheese, nuts, citrus, or greens.
core_ingredients: Acid such as vinegar or lemon juice
preparation_steps: Cook or roast the beets until tender, then cool and peel them.
Slice, dice, or wedge the beets.
Prepare a vinaigrette or other light dressing.
Combine with greens, cheese, fruit, or nuts if using.

nutrition.protein: Low, unless cheese or legumes are added
nutrition.fat: Low to moderate, depending on dressing and toppings

health_advice.benefits: Beets provide fiber and beneficial plant compounds, and the salad can be nutrient-dense when paired with vegetables and modest dressing.
health_advice.risks: Calories rise quickly if large amounts of cheese, candied nuts, or sweet dressings are used.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Keep the dressing balanced and use cheese or nuts in moderate amounts.

dietary_notes.note: Often naturally vegetarian and gluten-free. Vegan versions omit cheese and honey.

common_variations: Roasted beet and goat cheese salad
